headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: April 14, 2026**
article:
The Arizona Diamondbacks secured a thrilling 4-3 victory over the Baltimore Orioles, with Merrill Kelly earning the win and Paul Sewald notching the save. Ildemaro Vargas led the Diamondbacks' offense with a home run and three RBIs. The Washington Nationals edged the Pittsburgh Pirates 5-4, with CJ Abrams leading the offense and Mitchell Parker earning the win. Abrams hit three home runs, drove in two runs, and went 4 for 4 at the plate.

Several players have been impacted by injuries and availability. The San Diego Padres placed right-hander Nick Pivetta on the 15-day injured list due to right elbow inflammation, effective retroactive to April 13, 2026. The Arizona Diamondbacks also placed catcher Gabriel Moreno on the 10-day injured list retroactive to April 11, 2026, due to a left oblique strain. The Chicago Cubs placed right-hander Ethan Roberts on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 13, 2026, due to a right middle finger laceration.

The Philadelphia Phillies made a trade involving right-hander Griff McGarry, who was sent to the Los Angeles Dodgers in exchange for future considerations and international bonus pool money. The Cincinnati Reds traded first baseman Christian Encarnacion-Strand to the Baltimore Orioles for cash. The Chicago White Sox traded second baseman Lenyn Sosa to the Toronto Blue Jays for outfielder Jordan Rich and future considerations.

top_news.title: **Diamondbacks Edge Orioles in Thrilling 4-3 Victory**
top_news.summary: The Arizona Diamondbacks secured a narrow win over the Baltimore Orioles, with Merrill Kelly earning the win and Paul Sewald notching the save. Ildemaro Vargas led the Diamondbacks' offense with a home run and three RBIs.

top_news.title: **Royals Fall Short in Detroit**
top_news.summary: The Detroit Tigers edged the Kansas City Royals 2-1, securing their win. Dillon Dingler led the Tigers' offense with 2 hits and 1 RBI.

top_news.title: **Nationals Edge Pirates in Thrilling Finish**
top_news.summary: The Washington Nationals secured a 5-4 victory over the Pittsburgh Pirates, with CJ Abrams leading the offense and Mitchell Parker earning the win. Abrams hit 3 home runs, drove in 2 runs, and went 4 for 4 at the plate, while Parker pitched 2 scoreless innings with 5 strikeouts.

top_news.title: **Giants Fall Short in Cincinnati**
top_news.summary: The San Francisco Giants dropped a 1-2 decision to the Cincinnati Reds, with Willy Adames leading the Giants' offense with 2 hits, including a home run, and 1 RBI. Robbie Ray pitched 5 strong innings for the Giants, striking out 6 and allowing 2 earned runs.

top_news.title: **Padres' Pivetta Lands on Injured List**
top_news.summary: San Diego Padres right-hander Nick Pivetta has been placed on the 15-day injured list due to right elbow inflammation, effective retroactive to April 13, 2026.

top_news.title: **Phillies Make Roster Move Ahead of Opening Day**
top_news.summary: The Philadelphia Phillies have made a trade involving RHP Griff McGarry, who was sent to the Los Angeles Dodgers in exchange for future considerations and international bonus pool money.
